topic: Program 5 (Craft/Home/Mobile) at 0% interest up to OMR 20K — who qualifies and how to apply
format: Open with a question about a business idea the reader has been sitting on, then show the exact first step to fund it
post:
You've got a business idea. Maybe it's a home bakery, a mobile service, a small craft workshop. And it's still just sitting there, in your head, because you're not sure where the money comes from.

Short answer: Program 5.

Development Bank Oman's Program 5 covers Craft, Home, and Mobile businesses — up to OMR 20,000 at 0% interest. No hidden fees. No equity given away.

The part that trips most people up: you need a feasibility study before you can apply. A lot of applicants either skip it entirely, or spend OMR 150–300 on someone to write one that gets rejected anyway.

The study doesn't have to be complicated. It has to be structured the way DBO and Riyada actually review it — financials, market sizing, projected cash flow, sector fit. That's the difference between a study that moves forward and one that doesn't.

One more thing: if your Sanad office management system is a WhatsApp group and a spreadsheet, fix that first. Clean documentation and organised client records are what make an application look credible to reviewers.

Get the feasibility done. The funding follows from there.

You can build a bank-ready feasibility study free in about 10 minutes — it covers all 10 Development Bank Oman / Riyada programs: https://www.thesmartpro.io/feasibility-studio?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=feasibility

What's the one thing stopping you from submitting today?
